reference: finite-difference numerical solution (consistent with the -sin(pi x) IC)
metric: rel_l2_pct = relative L2 error vs FD (%); antisymmetry = mean|f(x)+f(-x)|/mean|f| (0=odd in x)

psinn.note: directly trained structured architecture (no distillation); evaluated artifact

clustering_tradeoff.note: 12-cluster reconstruction (eq. replace) of the directly trained net does NOT retain the full model's accuracy; a compact 12-cluster model at this accuracy requires the distillation-based clustering of Ref. [23], not used here

note: Psi-NN (structured, directly trained, 1937 params) and the 4x50 dense PINN (7851 params, matching Sec. IV.B.3) are trained identically on the same forward Burgers objective (physics+IC+BC) and scored against a finite-difference reference. The directly trained structured network is ~4.1x smaller than the dense PINN (7.6 KB vs 30.7 KB), ~6.0x more accurate (rel L2 5.0% vs 30.2%), and preserves the physical odd-in-x symmetry (0.006 vs 0.146) -- on the same training budget. Generic compression of the dense net never closes the gap (rel L2 >= 63% at every sparsity; pruning to structured scale collapses it toward a constant, antisymmetry -> 2.0). Aggressive post-training clustering of the structured net to a 12-cluster footprint does NOT retain that accuracy (see clustering_tradeoff); the compact 12-cluster model of Ref. [23] required distillation, not used here. Training is untuned per architecture; absolute errors would improve with per-model tuning, but the comparison holds the protocol fixed.
